item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel Expands Lebanon Offensive; Nations Appear Cool to Trump’s Oil Route Demands
item.description: The Israeli military said that it was escalating attacks against Iran-backed Hezbollah. China and other nations reacted tepidly to President Trump’s call for warships to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Whistleblower murder suspect is former elite officer, South African police say
item.description: Marius van der Merwe was shot dead three weeks after accusing police officers of killing a robbery suspect.

item.title: Starmer announces £53m support to help with heating oil costs
item.description: The money will be for "vulnerable" households who have faced a sharp rise in energy bills since the outbreak of the US-Israeli war with Iran.

item.title: Here is the latest.
Far-left and far-right gains throw French mainstream parties into a quandary
item.description: Awkward choices emerge for mainstream parties after the first round of mayoral elections.

item.title: 4 Palestinians Killed When Israeli Forces Open Fire on West Bank Family
item.description: Six members of a Palestinian family went out for a ride in the car. Only two made it back home.

item.title: Key Oscars moments as snubbed Chalamet becomes butt of jokes
item.description: Here's what happened inside the winners room and other insights from the biggest night in Hollywood.

item.title: FirstFT: Donald Trump warns European allies on Nato over Hormuz plea
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: wealthy individuals pull $10bn from private credit funds and a good night for One Battle After Another

item.title: Is Cheburashka, the Beloved Soviet-Born Character, Ruining Russia?
item.description: Instead of obsessing over the fictional Cheburashka, Russians should be focused on more important things like the rebirth of a Russian empire, influential conservatives say.

item.title: In Iraq, the U.S. Tried to Bring Allies on Board. Not in Iran.
item.description: This time, President Trump went to war without preparing the public, seeking U.N. approval or even consulting allies. But they will have to pick up the pieces.

item.title: Non-alcoholic beer and pet grooming added to UK inflation basket
item.description: ONS updates list of items used to measure price growth on back of changing spending habits

item.title: UK should avoid using subsidies to cap energy costs
item.description: Amid Iran war price shock, Labour should propose a better measure than the Tories’ ‘energy price guarantee’

item.title: Kpop Demon Hunters wins Oscars for best animated film and best original song
item.description: The Oscars are the latest in a string of accolades for the film, which include Golden Globe and Grammy awards.

item.title: Emirates reroutes flights after drone attack on Dubai’s airport
item.description: Flights from Manchester, Edinburgh and Dublin among those that returned to destinations on Monday

item.title: Intrigue, Power Plays and Rivalries: Inside the Rise of Mojtaba Khamenei
item.description: The weeklong fight over Iran’s next leader pitted the Revolutionary Guards against moderates. The generals won, but only over spirited resistance.

item.title: What Do Africans Think of Trump’s Travel Ban List?
item.description: The restrictions on half the continent have been called racist and unfair. “We don’t come to the United States because we’re running away,” one N.B.A. fan said.

item.title: Yale padlock maker to scrap CEO appointment in deal with activist investor
item.description: Fortune Brands staves off proxy battle with asset manager set up by Trian co-founder Ed Garden

item.title: Belgian prime minister calls for EU to normalise ties with Russia
item.description: Rightwing nationalist Bart De Wever challenges full support for Ukraine in quest for lower energy prices

item.title: French far right makes first-round gains in municipal elections
item.description: Local votes are crucial test of parties’ strength and appetite for alliances ahead of 2027 presidential contest

item.title: Laptop found in search for missing Brazilian woman
item.description: Police also believe a boat Vitoria Barreto may have taken is missing a buoyancy aid.

item.title: Marseille’s Tight Mayoral Race Is a Bellwether for France’s Future
item.description: Voters across France choose mayors this week. The far right is performing strongly in the country’s second city, making the contest there a test of national shifts.

item.title: Pakistani-Afghan War Takes Heavy Toll on Civilians
item.description: Pakistani airstrikes have killed at least 75 civilians and displaced 115,000 in Afghanistan, with both sides vowing escalation and no talks in sight.

item.title: 2022 redux?
item.description: War makes it possible.

item.title: TikTok and Meta risked safety to win algorithm arms race, whistleblowers say
item.description: Companies allowed more harmful content on user’s feeds, knowing their algorithms ran on outrage, BBC hears.

item.title: Starmer warned over assisted dying bill by more than 100 Labour MPs
item.description: More than 100 Labour MPs warn that failing to pass the legislation would undermine trust in politics.

item.title: India seeks greater ‘chemistry’ with EU capitals as ties deepen
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Arguments against an EU gas price cap

item.title: Ten killed in fire at India hospital intensive care unit
item.description: All the victims of the fire in Odisha state are patients, and 11 hospital staff are being treated for burns.

item.title: The Iran War’s Global Reach
item.description: The fighting is raising energy prices, hitting farmers and reshaping geopolitics. We look at the ripple effects worldwide.

item.title: Central banks set to pivot in a hawkish direction
item.description: Reaction to the supply shock will set the tone for the months ahead

item.title: Reeves must tackle ‘badly broken’ student loans, Cambridge chancellor says
item.description: Chris Smith criticises ‘absurd position’ graduates find themselves in and calls for more vocational education

item.title: The UK is struggling to achieve its aims on clean energy
item.description: Our economy remains highly fossil fuel-dependent partly because we are not electrifying in the desired way

item.title: Netanyahu’s rivals compete to outdo him over Iran
item.description: Israel’s opposition parties criticise PM’s management of war even if they express strong support for offensive

item.title: Retail investors pull billions from private capital’s credit gold mine
item.description: Flood of redemptions threatens to stall one of Wall Street’s most important sources of growth

item.title: Private credit is key to keep Main Street moving
item.description: Despite recent concerns, it helps sustain job creation and economic growth

item.title: India’s restaurants stop deep frying food as gas crisis bites
item.description: South Asia reels from global energy crisis sparked by the US-Israeli war on Iran

item.title: Energy groups press UK to boost gas storage after Iran war price shock
item.description: Middle East conflict renews focus on Britain’s resilience to extreme events

item.title: Business leaders call for housebuilding push to boost London economy
item.description: Construction starts fell by more than 80% over the past decade, according to research firm Molior

item.title: FT’s Online MBA ranking 2026: methodology, key and entry criteria
item.description: How the Online MBA ranking was compiled

item.title: Can the last bamboo artisans of Hong Kong survive the modern age?
item.description: The material is an inescapable part of the city’s landscape and heritage — but its future is precarious

item.title: This is what happened on March 15.
Iran taking steps to prevent anti-establishment protests, Tehran residents tell BBC
item.description: Residents describe new security checkpoints around the capital, where they say people are stopped and searched.

item.title: Firms to be paid to hire unemployed young people
item.description: Payments of £3,000 for each 18-24 year old given a job are among proposals to tackle youth unemployment being announced later.

item.title: The War Is Making It Harder to Keep the Lights On, 2,000 Miles Away
item.description: Bangladesh is taking steps to conserve electricity, which its factories need to keep stitching together the world’s clothing.

item.title: How did Oscar winner Kpop Demon Hunters win hearts the world over?
item.description: We find out how the award-winning, demon-busting crooners have cast a spell world over.

item.title: One-off £2,000 grant gives care leavers head start, study finds
item.description: Participants were less likely to become homeless or spend time in hospital or prison, researchers say.

item.title: Fears of two-tier health system as more turn to private care, says watchdog
item.description: The patient watchdog warns of two-tier service as polling shows numbers paying for care is on the rise.

item.title: Ukraine's urgent fight on the financial frontline
item.description: The war-torn country is battling to secure crucial funding from the IMF and EU, as well as putting up taxes.

item.title: 'We will go wherever they hide': Rooting out IS in Somalia
item.description: Somalia became a key part of IS's global network after fighters were driven out of strongholds in Syria and Iraq.

item.title: What Iranians are being told about the war
item.description: Iranian state media's coverage of the war includes inflations of enemy casualties and digital manipulation intended to glorify Iran.

item.title: Sinner matches Federer & Djokovic with Indian Wells win
item.description: Jannik Sinner said it "means a lot to me" after he becomes the youngest man to complete the set of hard-court trophies with victory over Daniil Medvedev in the Indian Wells final.

item.title: Labour calls for tax investigation into Reform's Tice
item.description: It follows a newspaper report that Tice had avoided corporation tax worth hundreds of thousands of pounds.

item.title: Desperation in Cuba Ignites Unusual Acts of Defiance
item.description: A protest in the city of Morón in central Cuba culminated in fiery vandalism at the local Communist Party headquarters.

item.title: Entering War’s Third Week, Trump Faces Stark Choices
item.description: As the conflict with Iran expands and intensifies, President Trump’s options — to fight on, or to move toward declaring victory and pulling back — both carry deeply problematic consequences.

item.title: ‘Doctor Who’ Fans Have Fresh Chance to Time Travel With Found Episodes
item.description: Two unearthed episodes, which were discovered in film canisters wrapped in plastic bags among the possessions of a dead collector in England, were restored by BBC archivists.

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Sunday
item.description: Five missiles struck the Baghdad International Airport and injured four people. Israeli and Iranian attacks continued as global economic concerns about the war mounted.

item.title: Iran captain latest footballer to drop asylum bid, Iranian state media says
item.description: Five of the seven members of the women's football delegation who originally sought to stay in Australia have now chosen to return home.

item.title: Ugandan opposition leader tells BBC he fled abroad fearing for his life
item.description: Bobi Wine says from an undisclosed location: "It was clear that the regime wanted to eliminate me."

item.title: Why the Iran crisis caught Europe flat-footed
item.description: The EU suffers the war’s fallout, but can do little to cut it short

item.title: Starmer speaks to Trump about importance of reopening Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The leaders discussed the need to "end the disruption to global shipping", No 10 says.

item.title: Sweden detains captain of Russian shadow fleet ship
item.description: The skipper of the Sea Owl One is suspected of producing forged documents when it was boarded by coast guard.

item.title: How Iranians are evading internet blocks to contact family abroad
item.description: Iranians are finding tech-savvy ways to get through the regime's restrictions on phone and internet connections.

item.title: The Iran war may be about to escalate
item.description: Gulf states could join the conflict

item.title: Open-source intelligence shuts down
item.description: Satellite images of the Middle East are suddenly disappearing

item.title: French police detain brothers over 'lethal and antisemitic' plot
item.description: A loaded gun, hydrochloric acid and an Islamic State flag were found in their car, prosecutors say.

item.title: Severe flooding kills 66 in Kenya, as heavy rains continue
item.description: Eleven people were rescued overnight after a minibus taxi got stuck in rising floodwaters in Nairobi.

item.title: A Timeline of the Fraught Relationship Between Iran and the U.S.
item.description: The governments of both countries have repeatedly cast the other as evil, perpetuating a cycle that has culminated in the present war.

item.title: Nordic Leaders Praise Carney as They Discuss Arctic Security
item.description: Canada’s prime minister got a warm welcome from leaders still smarting about President Trump’s remarks two months ago about taking over Greenland.

item.title: 'Best racing ever' or 'a joke'? The row over the new F1
item.description: Have F1's new rules produced a "battery world championship" or "the best racing ever"? Lewis Hamilton, Max Verstappen and Fernando Alonso sum up the paradox at the heart of the sport.

item.title: What Nitish Kumar did for Bihar, India’s poorest state
item.description: The state’s longest-serving chief minister raised voters’ expectations of their politicians

item.title: Spain-Argentina Finalissima in Qatar cancelled
item.description: The Finalissima between Argentina and Spain set to be held in Qatar this month is cancelled because of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: UK must build own nuclear missiles to end reliance on US, say Lib Dems
item.description: Sir Ed Davey is calling on the government to start work on a "fully independent British nuclear deterrent".

item.title: Zelensky accuses EU allies of 'blackmail' in oil pipeline row
item.description: Volodymyr Zelensky says restoring the flow of Russian oil via Ukraine into the EU would be like lifting sanctions on Russia.

item.title: We will intervene on energy bills if necessary, says Miliband
item.description: Oil and gas prices have surged due to the US-Israel war in Iran, with fears over the cost of living.

item.title: Police-style powers to tackle fly-tippers being considered
item.description: The move would allow officers to search premises without a warrant, seize assets and arrest those suspected of criminality.

item.title: Israeli forces kill Palestinian couple and two of their children in occupied West Bank
item.description: Mohammed, aged five, and Othman, aged seven, were killed alongside their parents, the Palestinian health ministry says.

item.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
item.description: Conflict has spread across the Middle East since the US and Israel launched wide-ranging strikes on Iran on 28 February.

item.title: As Rockets Fly Overhead, Residents of Israel’s Border City Stay Underground
item.description: The Israeli government evacuated Kiryat Shmona during the last round of fighting with Hezbollah in 2023. Residents who were told it was safe to return are again under fire.

item.title: US names six crew killed in refuelling plane crash in Iraq
item.description: The aircraft was on a combat mission as part of ongoing US operations against Iran when it crashed in western Iraq on Thursday.

item.title: Caddy faces 'hardest weeks' on New Zealand trek
item.description: Dougie Haynes, originally from Oxford, says the hardest section of his trek comes with "spectacular views".

item.title: Iceland’s Chief ‘Lava Cooler’ Is Bracing for the Next Volcanic Eruption
item.description: Helgi Hjorleifsson, a firefighter, is a leader in a national experiment to steer rivers of lava away from important sites. Some called it crazy, but it worked.

item.title: An eccentric new friend entered her life. Then things took a strange, expensive turn
item.description: What began as a dog-sitting favour ended with boutique owner Bridget Hutchcroft being owed more than £1.6m.

item.title: Will Trump help or hinder Zimbabwe's white farmers in their compensation battle?
item.description: They have engaged a lobbying firm with close ties to the US president to argue their case.

item.title: BBC visits aftermath of Israeli strike on Lebanon that killed family as IDF targets Hezbollah
item.description: A relative tells BBC those killed were civilians and not Hezbollah operatives, but the Israel Defense Forces says it was targeting "terrorist infrastructure".

item.title: Cuban protesters ransack Communist office as energy crisis deepens
item.description: Discontent is mounting over rolling blackouts and shortages of food, fuel and medicine, exacerbated by a US blockade.

item.title: Trump urges UK and other nations to send warships to Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US president says he hopes China, France, Japan and South Korea will also send ships to defend the key oil shipping route.

item.title: German philosopher and social critic Jürgen Habermas dies at 96
item.description: One the most influential thinkers in post-war Germany, he linked philosophy and political action throughout his life.

item.title: Rescuers blame weather and 'underprepared skiers' for rise in Alps avalanche deaths
item.description: The BBC joins a French Alps rescue team as the number of skiers killed this season passes 100.

item.title: Former Labour minister dies after cancer diagnosis
item.description: The ex-Oldham MP Phil Woolas battled brain cancer for more than a year, his family says.

item.title: Why has the US targeted Iran's Kharg Island?
item.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

item.title: Hamas urges key ally Iran to halt attacks on Gulf states
item.description: The Palestinian group also affirms Tehran's right to defend itself from "aggression" by the US and Israel.

item.title: More US Marines and warships being moved to Middle East, reports say
item.description: The reinforcements will come from an amphibious ready group that is usually based in Japan, an official told CBS, the BBC's US partner.

item.title: Explosion at Amsterdam Jewish school 'a deliberate attack' says mayor
item.description: Security had been increased at Jewish public institutions following an earlier incident in Rotterdam.

item.title: Pete Hegseth’s Holy War
item.description: Christian nationalism was once a fringe ideology in America. The Secretary of War has ushered it into the heart of U.S. military force.

item.title: Chancellor to offer support over rising heating oil costs
item.description: Rachel Reeves says the Treasury is also looking at "different options" to help households most vulnerable to soaring energy bills.

item.title: Doomed hereditary peers spy chance to stay in the Lords
item.description: The Tories are deciding which hereditary peers to keep after being offered 15 seats in a compromise deal.

item.title: Fears for press freedom as tycoon takes control of East Africa's largest media house
item.description: Rostam Aziz's purchase raises questions about continued journalistic independence in the region.

item.title: Is Dubai's glossy image under threat? Not everyone thinks so
item.description: The city has been considered a safe haven by expats, tourists and influencers - but that's now being tested.

item.title: 'Tigers and flies': Millions of officials later, why is Xi's corruption purge still going?
item.description: Critics say Xi's purges reveal a ruthless drive for absolute loyalty and total control of the military.

item.title: Ethics adviser rejects Tory call for inquiry into PM over Mandelson appointment
item.description: Sir Laurie Magnus said the relevant process appeared to have been followed when Lord Mandelson was made ambassador.

item.title: Under drone fire, exiled Kurds wait to confront Iranian regime
item.description: A group of fighters based in Iraq say they are "ready to go home", as they face drone attacks from Tehran.

item.title: Cuba confirms negotiations with US as country faces effects of oil blockade
item.description: Cuba's president confirmed the talks in a national broadcast as the country also said it would release 51 prisoners in "goodwill" gesture.

item.title: First image emerges of Andrew, Mandelson and Epstein together
item.description: The image shows Lord Mandelson and the former prince in bathrobes sitting on wooden decking with Epstein.

item.title: Petrol retailers in row with government over 'rip off' accusations
item.description: They briefly threatened to pull out of a government meeting, accusing ministers of using "inflammatory language" over rising fuel prices.

item.title: UK economy flatlines in January as people cut back on eating out
item.description: Analysts had been expecting 0.2% growth for the UK economy at the beginning of the year.

item.title: Why has Trump eased sanctions on Russian oil - and will it help Putin?
item.description: The US said easing sanctions on Russian oil would provide only a limited financial boost to Putin.

item.title: France returns sacred 'talking drum' looted during colonial rule to Ivory Coast
item.description: The move forms part of a broader process of returning cultural artefacts to African countries that started in 2017.‎

item.title: Iranians react to new supreme leader's first address
item.description: People in Iran are questioning who's really in charge after Mojtaba Khamenei's first address came via a presenter on state TV.

item.title: As hopes of regime change in Iran fade, Netanyahu faces political test
item.description: Israeli leaders are framing the bombing campaign as having transformed the Middle East in their favour as pressure to end the conflict builds.

item.title: UK urges allies to keep up pressure on Russia as US eases sanctions
item.description: Ministers say the UK will not be following the US by loosening sanctions on some Russian oil.

item.title: Games with loot boxes to get minimum 16 age rating across Europe
item.description: The new changes by the Pan-European Game Information age-ratings body (PEGI) will start from June.

item.title: Farage regrets taking control of 'bankrupt' council
item.description: The Reform UK leader says he wishes the party "hadn't bothered" to take control in Worcestershire.

item.title: Ghana star to deny rape charges, UK court hears
item.description: Partey is to appear at crown court next month, charged with raping a woman in December 2020.

item.title: 'No-one will hire women' - India's top court rejects menstrual leave petition
item.description: The subject has polarised India for long, though some states and private companies offer menstrual leaves.
